title: Rivian Q3 2023 Earnings Article
text: Rivian Automotive's stock gained 4.4% in after-hours trading following its third-quarter 2023 earnings release. Revenue reached $1.34 billion, surpassing the Wall Street estimate of $1.31 billion. Revenue climbed 149% year-over-year and 19% quarter-over-quarter. Adjusted loss per share was $1.19, better than analysts' projected loss of $1.33 per share. The company produced 16,304 vehicles, representing 17% growth from Q2, and vehicle deliveries totaled 15,564 units, up 23% sequentially. Operating losses narrowed 19% compared to the year-ago quarter. Operating cash burn improved 36% to $877 million. Balance sheet strengthened with $9.13 billion in liquid assets. The manufacturer increased its 2023 production forecast to 54,000 vehicles (from 52,000) and improved adjusted EBITDA guidance to negative $4.0 billion, from negative $4.2 billion. Rivian ended its exclusive supply agreement with Amazon for its electric delivery van business, opening opportunities for broader commercial vehicle sales.
